title: The Suppressive and Emergent Phenomenon: H as a Control Surface
description:
Live streaming and VTuber communities constitute the largest naturally occurring laboratory for parasocial κ-dynamics currently observable. Tens of millions of viewers form high-coupling bonds with performers they have never met; for many, these relationships constitute the primary vector through which healthy-state representations enter their generative model for the first time. The MBD framework — which models personality as a state-space system updated by lived experience — predicts this outcome precisely: high-κ social inputs from a trusted source can expand Hagent into previously unrepresented state space regardless of whether the relationship is co-present. The phenomenon is not parasocial pathology. It is the Re-zeroing Protocol running at scale, mediated by broadcasting infrastructure instead of physical proximity.

Paper 7 of the Memory as Baseline Deviation series established the endemic baseline as a naturally occurring calibration failure: B(0) constructed during chronic disruption, with the Horizon H = Haccessible \ Hagent denoting states that are neurobiologically reachable but absent from the agent’s generative model. That paper treated this condition as arising from circumstance. This paper examines a harder claim: the same calibration failure can be produced deliberately. An agent — platform, institution, abuser — that controls which states a population occupies can engineer H over time. The mathematics do not distinguish between endemic baseline arising from circumstance and endemic baseline arising from design.

Four attack vectors are formalized with their population-level signatures and detection criteria. V1 (Isolation from Knowledge) suppresses informational inputs that would enable Hagent expansion. V2 (Episodic Erasure) degrades or falsifies the episodic record. V3 (Coupling Collapse) drives the social coupling coefficient κ below the critical threshold at which therapeutic relationships can form. V4 (Re-zeroing Suppression) targets the corrective mechanism directly. All four are observable in the parasocial economy: deplatforming, algorithmic isolation, community weaponization, and content suppression each instantiate a vector.

A critical coupling threshold κc is derived by analogy with the Kuramoto synchronization threshold: below κc, collective re-zeroing fails; above it, high-κ fandom communities can expand each other’s Hagent faster than the suppressive mechanism can contract it. This is a testable, falsifiable prediction with computational labs provided in the repository.
